Class OutboxAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.spring.OutboxAutoConfiguration
@AutoConfiguration(after=DataSourceAutoConfiguration.class, afterName={"org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.MetricsAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.CompositeMeterRegistryAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.export.simple.SimpleMetricsExportAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.export.prometheus.PrometheusMetricsExportAutoConfiguration","io.opentelemetry.instrumentation.spring.autoconfigure.OpenTelemetryAutoConfiguration"}) @ConditionalOnClass({Outbox.class,JdbcOutbox.class}) @ConditionalOnSingleCandidate(DataSource.class) @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="io.github.lobofoltran.outbox", name="enabled", matchIfMissing=true) @EnableConfigurationProperties(OutboxProperties.class) public class OutboxAutoConfiguration extends Object
Auto-configures an Outbox bean backed by JdbcOutbox.

The bean is published only when:

  • outbox-jdbc is on the classpath (so JdbcOutbox resolves),
  • a single DataSource bean exists in the context,
  • the property io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.enabled is missing or true,
  • no other Outbox bean was already registered by the user.

The connectionSupplier delegates to DataSourceUtils.getConnection(dataSource) so the INSERT participates in whichever transaction Spring has bound to the current thread.

Customization

Adopters can register one or more JdbcOutboxBuilderCustomizer beans to tweak the auto-configured builder (custom IdGenerator, deterministic Clock, explicit OutboxDialect, …) without having to hand-roll a replacement Outbox bean. Customizers are invoked in @Order-aware sequence, after OutboxProperties have been applied to the builder and immediately before build().

Decoration order

When both observability decorators are eligible, the resulting bean stack is: 

TracedOutbox(MeteredOutbox(JdbcOutbox))

This ordering is enforced by both BeanPostProcessor implementations directly implementing Ordered. Spring runs ordered BeanPostProcessors with the lowest order value first, so the metrics BPP carries the lower value (Ordered.LOWEST_PRECEDENCE - 200) and runs first, seeing the bare JdbcOutbox; the tracing BPP carries Ordered.LOWEST_PRECEDENCE - 100 and runs last, wrapping the already-metered delegate. The effect is that the metric increments and the OpenTelemetry span share the same logical operation: the metric is recorded inside the producer span.

Both decorators are opt-out via the nested metrics.enabled and tracing.enabled switches. The metrics decorator additionally requires a MeterRegistry bean; the tracing decorator is applied whenever the OpenTelemetry API is on the classpath and resolves the OpenTelemetry instance from a Spring bean if present, falling back to GlobalOpenTelemetry.get() (which is what opentelemetry-sdk-extension-autoconfigure and the OpenTelemetry Java Agent populate).

When metrics.enabled stays at its default (true) but no MeterRegistry bean is visible at Outbox post-processing time, the auto-config logs a one-shot WARN naming the missed decorator and listing the two remediations (register a registry, or set metrics.enabled=false). The metrics decorator is silently skipped only when the adopter has explicitly opted out via the property. Tracing has no equivalent warning because it always falls back to the no-op global SDK.

Autoconfig ordering

The nested MetricsConfiguration declares an explicit "after" relationship to the Micrometer autoconfigurations that contribute MeterRegistry beans ( MetricsAutoConfiguration / SimpleMetricsExportAutoConfiguration / PrometheusMetricsExportAutoConfiguration). This ordering ensures the registry bean is already available when our BeanPostProcessor starts wrapping Outbox beans, so the runtime ObjectProvider.getIfAvailable() probe inside the BPP resolves the registry rather than racing the autoconfig that creates it. The tracing entry below was kept for the same parity reason even though tracing no longer participates in @ConditionalOnBean.

The autoconfig FQNs are referenced via afterName (string form) rather than after (class literal) because outbox-spring must not compile-depend on the optional Micrometer / OpenTelemetry autoconfig modules. afterName silently no-ops when a referenced class is absent from the classpath, which is the exact semantic required for these optional relationships.

Health

If Spring Boot Actuator is on the classpath an OutboxHealthIndicator is registered under the name outbox. It is opt-out via health.enabled and respects the standard management.health.outbox.enabled switch.

Since:
0.1.0

    outbox(DataSource dataSource, OutboxProperties properties, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<JdbcOutboxBuilderCustomizer> customizers)
    Creates the default Outbox bean (a JdbcOutbox) wired to the single DataSource in the context, with optional JdbcOutboxBuilderCustomizer hooks applied before JdbcOutbox.Builder.build().

  • Constructor Details

    • OutboxAutoConfiguration

      public OutboxAutoConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • outbox

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean(Outbox.class) public Outbox outbox(DataSource dataSource, OutboxProperties properties, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<JdbcOutboxBuilderCustomizer> customizers)
      Creates the default Outbox bean (a JdbcOutbox) wired to the single DataSource in the context, with optional JdbcOutboxBuilderCustomizer hooks applied before JdbcOutbox.Builder.build().
      Parameters:
      dataSource - the DataSource the outbox writes through.
      properties - the bound OutboxProperties.
      customizers - ordered customizers contributed by the application.
      Returns:
      the configured Outbox bean.
      Since:
      0.1.0
